title: Mass. high school pitcher Brody Bumila’s fastball tops 100 mph, draws crowd of MLB scouts
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Brody Bumila, a Massachusetts high school pitcher, hit 100 mph with his fastball, attracting nearly 50 MLB scouts.
Bumila struck out nine batters across three innings, allowing no hits, but Hopkinton eventually won 6-5.
Bumila, ranked 69th by Baseball America, is committed to the University of Texas but may consider going pro.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (April 3, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Hawksmoor, a renowned British steakhouse, is opening its third U.S. location in Boston's Seaport in Fall 2026.
Jazz Urbane Cafe, a jazz club and restaurant featuring Southern and global cuisine, will open in Nubian Square in May 2026.
NAYA, a Lebanese-inspired fast-casual chain, plans to open its latest location in Framingham by late summer 2026.

title: Daughter runs Boston Marathon to honor dad with rare vision disorder
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: London Tuma, 22, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her father diagnosed with Usher syndrome.
London's father, Jon Tuma, has Usher syndrome type 2C, affecting his hearing and vision since age two.
She is part of Team Eye and Ear, supporting research at Mass Eye and Ear for Usher syndrome treatment.

title: BU rally pushes back on Pride flag removals, calls for policy change
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University community protested the removal of Pride flags, urging a change in signage policy outside administrative offices.
The group presented a petition with over 2,000 signatures to Boston University officials during the rally.
University policy restricting outward-facing signage sparked controversy over free speech and LGBTQ representation on campus.

title: She knit an MBTA-themed sweater inspired by her commutes. The internet is loving it.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Megan Lam, Boston-based designer, created a hand-knit sweater inspired by MBTA commuter and subway lines.
The sweater features trains from the MBTA's commuter rail, Orange, Green, and Red lines.
Lam's Instagram debut of the sweater received overwhelming positive feedback and highlights her unique design approach.

title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: 18-year-old Emmie Laudano from Wellesley will run her first Boston Marathon to support the Tierney Learning Center.
Emmie has joined the Dreamfar training program, preparing for the marathon by running up Heartbreak Hill each Saturday.
Funds raised by Emmie's run will support Tierney Learning Center's educational programs for Boston children and teens.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com released a local news quiz covering the latest trending stories in Greater Boston.
Peter Chianca has been the general assignment editor at Boston.com since 2019.
The quiz is designed to test readers' knowledge of recent news events in Greater Boston.

title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marco Sturm criticized the Boston Bruins for not respecting the injury-depleted Florida Panthers in their 2-1 loss.
Despite a late push with 14 third-period shots, the Bruins couldn't capitalize against Panthers' goalie Sergei Bobrovsky.
Boston Bruins' playoff odds remain at 95% despite the loss, ahead of the upcoming playoffs in a few weeks.

title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied of Reading, Mass., sentenced to eight months for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts.
Bied imported illegal wildlife parts from Cameroon and Indonesia, violating U.S. wildlife laws including the Lacey Act.
Bied to pay a $75,000 fine and serve two years of supervised release after his prison sentence.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black, 22, was convicted of first-degree murder for fatally stabbing 19-year-old Milteer Hendricks in 2023.
The incident occurred at an apartment in Falmouth, where Black attacked Hendricks with the help of four others.
Hendricks died after being stabbed in the chest; Black was arrested in Taunton two days later.
